breaking_news.title: Juniper Networks PTX Routers Hit by Critical Unauthenticated RCE Flaw
breaking_news.summary:
Juniper Networks released an emergency patch for a critical remote code execution vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-21902 affecting PTX Series routers. The flaw resides in the On-Box Anomaly Detection framework of Junos OS Evolved and allows unauthenticated attackers to execute code with root privileges.

This vulnerability is especially dangerous because these devices often sit in the middle of the network, acting as traffic vantage points and control centers. If exploited, it could lead to stealthy interception of data flows and easy pivots into adjacent networks, compromising critical infrastructure globally.

breaking_news.title: North Korean ScarCruft Group Deploys New Tools to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
breaking_news.summary:
The North Korean threat actor known as ScarCruft has been attributed to a fresh set of tools including a backdoor that uses Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control communications. Researchers also identified an implant that uses removable media to relay commands and breach air-gapped networks, complicating traditional defense strategies.

This campaign, codenamed Ruby Jumper by Zscaler ThreatLabz, involves the deployment of malware designed to move data between internet-connected and air-gapped systems. The sophistication of these tools highlights the evolving capabilities of state-sponsored actors targeting high-value government and corporate secrets.

breaking_news.title: CISA Warns of Dormant RESURGE Malware Exploiting Ivanti Zero-Day
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has released new details about RESURGE, a malicious implant used in zero-day attacks exploiting CVE-2025-0282. This vulnerability allows threat actors to breach Ivanti Connect Secure devices and maintain persistent access within corporate networks.

The warning highlights that the malware can remain dormant on devices, making detection difficult for organizations relying on standard scanning tools. This poses a significant risk to critical infrastructure and enterprise networks that utilize Ivanti's virtual network access solutions.

breaking_news.title: South Korea Tax Office Apologizes After Leaking Crypto Wallet Seed Phrase
breaking_news.summary:
South Korea’s National Tax Service accidentally exposed the mnemonic recovery phrase of a seized cryptocurrency wallet in an official press release. This critical error allowed hackers to access the wallet and steal 6.4 billion won worth of cryptocurrency shortly after the disclosure.

The incident marks a significant embarrassment for the agency, which had previously celebrated busting tax dodgers and seizing the assets. It underscores the severe risks associated with handling digital assets and the need for stricter protocols in government cybersecurity operations.

breaking_news.title: Thousands of Public Google Cloud API Keys Exposed with Gemini Access
breaking_news.summary:
New research has found that Google Cloud API keys, typically designated as project identifiers for billing purposes, could be abused to authenticate to sensitive Gemini endpoints. Truffle Security discovered nearly 3,000 Google API keys embedded in client-side code that could grant access to private data.

This issue stems from a silent change in the status of Google Cloud Platform API keys which the company neglected to tell developers about. The exposure affects major financial institutions, security companies, and even Google itself, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or billing abuse.

breaking_news.title: Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million User Accounts
breaking_news.summary:
A data breach at Canadian Tire exposed personal data from over 38 million accounts, including contact details and encrypted passwords. The incident occurred in an e-commerce database and was identified on October 2, 2025, though details are only recently publicized.

This marks one of the largest retail data breaches in Canada, raising serious concerns about customer privacy and the security of sensitive personal information. While in-store transactions were unaffected, the scale of the compromise demands immediate attention from affected customers and regulators.

breaking_news.title: Aeternum Botnet Hides Commands in Polygon Smart Contracts for Resilience
breaking_news.summary:
Qrator Labs researchers uncovered Aeternum, a botnet that runs its command-and-control infrastructure through smart contracts on the Polygon blockchain. By decentralizing its C2, the malware avoids traditional server-based takedowns and becomes far harder to disrupt or shut down.

This approach makes Aeternum’s C2 infrastructure effectively permanent and resistant to traditional takedown methods, significantly increasing its persistence in the wild. The use of public blockchain networks for malicious command execution represents a novel evolution in botnet architecture.

breaking_news.title: ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents
breaking_news.summary:
OpenClaw has fixed a high-severity security issue that, if successfully exploited, could have allowed a malicious website to connect to a locally running artificial intelligence agent. The vulnerability lives in the core system itself, requiring no plugins or user-installed extensions to trigger.

Researchers found that modern browsers allow external websites to open WebSocket connections to local services, enabling unauthorized device pairing and password brute-forcing. This flaw highlights the security risks of trusting localhost connections in modern web-based AI development environments.

breaking_news.title: French DIY E-tailer ManoMano Admits Customer Data Stolen in Third-Party Attack
breaking_news.summary:
French online marketplace ManoMano is warning customers their personal data was siphoned off after a cyberattack hit one of its customer support subcontractors. Hackers claim they helped themselves to over 37 million accounts during the January hit, though the company initially admitted to a smaller number.

The incident highlights the risks of third-party vendor compromises in the e-commerce sector, where a single breach can impact millions of users across multiple countries. Criminals are already claiming the haul is far larger than the company's carefully worded notice suggests.

breaking_news.title: Iran’s Internet Near-Totally Blacked Out Amid US and Israeli Strikes
breaking_news.summary:
Iran experienced a near-total internet blackout as Israel and the U.S. launched strikes, according to NetBlocks. Network data indicated a near-total nationwide blackout with national connectivity down to about 4% of normal levels during the military operations.

This incident matches measures used during last year's war with Israel, demonstrating the use of internet shutdowns as a tactical tool in modern conflict. The disruption affects all sectors of the economy and society, preventing communication and access to critical services.
